paper.title: The runaway nature and origin of {\alpha} Crucis system

extraction.summary: The paper studies the alpha Crucis system (HD 108248, HD 108249, HD 108250) and explicitly concludes it is not a runaway star and is gravitationally bound to its parent cluster in the Scorpius-Centaurus association. No Galactic-unbound or HVS candidates are discussed.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The alpha Crucis system (HD 108248, HD 108249, HD 108250), the sole subject of the paper, investigated for runaway nature via kinematics and bow-shock analysis.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly concludes the alpha Crucis system is not runaway, has not escaped from its parent cluster, is gravitationally bound to other cluster members, and moves with a peculiar velocity of only ~1.3 km/s relative to the cluster—far below the runaway threshold. The bow shock is attributed to large-scale ISM motions, not stellar velocity. The paper does not present any object as possibly unbound from the Galaxy.

source_refs.context: Conclusion stating the alpha Crucis system is not runaway, has not escaped its parent cluster, is gravitationally bound, and the bow shock is unaligned due to ISM motions
Relative velocity section showing peculiar velocity of ~1.3 km/s, well below the 30 km/s runaway threshold
Parent cluster search showing the system is gravitationally bound to cluster members in the Scorpius-Centaurus association
